title: Five More Minutes
lyrics:
[opening]
*(Clock beeps—fast beat!)*
Mom: "Bus in twenty!"
Me: "Five more minutes—one last try!"

[verse 1]
Blanket webs on my toes,
Clock glows blue like bad-guy nose.
Doc Ock wins—just one more go,
If I get up, my hero’s slow!

[parent response]
Mom: "Why?"

[verse 2]
Pillow’s a trampoline, bounce so high,
Bus is a rocket—gonna miss the sky!
If I don’t win, the city burns,
Mom’s the bad guy—no hero turns!

[parent response]
Mom: "What?"

[chorus]
Five More Minutes!
Five More Minutes!
I’M Not Done Saving The World Yet!
Five More Minutes!

[verse 3]
I’ll shrink small, ride my clock,
Fly through plugs—stop the shock!
Bus driver’s Venom—took my seat,
Cereal’s soggy—still can’t eat!

*(Bus honks—loud and mean)*
Mom: "Shoes. Now."

[chorus]
Five More Minutes—
Five More—
...okay, fine, I’m...
*(yawns, face-plants)*
...chasing the bus...
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, guitar and drums, 117 BPM, strong bass, punchy beat, bouncy groovy infectious singalong, impossible to sit still, makes you move
scene: The blue digital clock on the nightstand glows 7:05 AM as you yank the Spider-Man comforter over your head, muffling Mom’s voice: *"Time to get up, sweetie—bus comes in twenty."* Your bare foot kicks the wall, and you groan, *"Five more minutes."*
cover_description: Blue clock glows 7:05, Spider-Man comforter half over a kid’s head, one bare foot kicking the wall.
rhyme_family: in it, in it, spin it, win it, bin it, grin it, tin it, skin it, kin it, begin it, thin it, pin it, fin it, din it, whinin’, sippin’, tippin’, flippin’, glisten, listen
